mechanism_narrative: FEDFUNDS has been held flat at 5.25% for multiple consecutive months after a prolonged tightening cycle, signaling the Fed has reached a terminal rate. Markets anticipate the next move is a cut rather than a hike, compressing forward discount rate expectations and lifting equity valuations over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS unchanged at 5.25% for several months as of 2006-11
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS-pause duration and 60-day forward SPX return stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or returns following pauses are not significantly different from baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below a threshold decline of 20% year-over-year signals a sharp deceleration in residential construction demand, which precedes broader consumer demand weakness and a negative equity market reaction over the subsequent month as investors price in slower growth.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1772k units in 2006-09, down substantially from 2005 peak levels above 2000k
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following HOUST YoY declines >20% are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains at cycle-high levels (>=5%) simultaneously with a sustained decline in HOUST and a rising UNRATE trend, the joint signal indicates tightening credit conditions are transmitting into housing and labor markets, raising recession probability and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. Neither series alone captures this transmission; the combination is required.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% with HOUST declining while UNRATE remains at cycle-low 4.4% as of 2006-11
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with emerging housing weakness
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS-high/HOUST-falling/UNRATE-rising signal fails to produce negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered periods, or the interaction term adds no explanatory power (delta Rank IC < 0.02) versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) reflects resilient labor demand that supports household income and consumption, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month as earnings expectations remain supported.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135844k thousand persons in 2006-10, consistent with continued expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward SPX return falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime where UNRATE sits near cyclical lows (below 4.5%), tight labor markets support consumer spending power and corporate revenue visibility, producing modest positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as risk appetite remains supported by low unemployment.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% in 2006-10, near cyclical lows
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns during UNRATE<4.5% periods are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) or the mechanism reverses sign when UNRATE approaches historical cyclical trough levels.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in the 3-month annualized growth rate of INDPRO below 1% signals weakening industrial output momentum, foreshadowing softer corporate earnings and triggering a negative S&P 500 reaction over the subsequent month as growth expectations are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 113.1336 in 2006-09, with growth decelerating from earlier 2006 pace
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO 3-month growth falls below 1%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects robust consumer demand that supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 363673 million dollars in 2006-10, showing continued growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing RSAFS momentum and 5-day forward SPX return stays below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation growth moderates toward the Fed's implicit comfort zone while FEDFUNDS remains on hold, markets interpret this combination as a successful soft landing, reducing the perceived risk of further tightening and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The effect depends on both series moving together, not on either alone.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 202.7 in 2006-09 with FEDFUNDS steady at 5.25%, suggesting inflation stabilization alongside a policy pause
regime_hint: soft landing transition after tightening cycle
failure_condition: The joint moderating-CPI/steady-FEDFUNDS signal fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC (<0.02) over single-series CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS models.
